name: Sessions & devices — approval frames
description: Contract-freeze UX artifacts for active-session/device management: list every signed-in device (browser, OS, location, last-seen, this-device flagged), sign out a single session (including the caller's own current session), and sign out everywhere else. Revocation is enforced server-side. Approving a flow approves its component/package plan before UI implementation. frontend-test-engineer drives Playwright against these via the data-frame / data-* hooks. No provider name appears in any consumer surface.

frames.label: (01) Active sessions
frames.summary: List of active sessions from GET /sessions with the current session flagged, device/OS/geo/last-seen metadata. Includes only-this-device, loading, load-error, and null-metadata (unknown device) states.
frames.acceptanceNotes: Rows come from SessionDevice[]; current:true renders the This-device badge and no destructive default. Null device/browser/os/geo render honest fallbacks ('Unknown device', 'Location unavailable'). lastSeenAt formats via RelativeTime. 'Sign out everywhere else' is hidden when only the current session exists.

frames.label: (02) Sign out one device
frames.summary: Confirm revoking one session. Distinct path for revoking the caller's OWN current session (self-logout warning -> redirect to /login). Includes revoked-success and revoke-failed states.
frames.acceptanceNotes: DELETE /sessions/{id} (idempotent). Revoking a non-current session shows a success and refreshes the list. Revoking data-current='true' warns it ends the local session, then on confirm redirects to /login (SelfRevokeNotice).

frames.label: (03) Sign out everywhere else
frames.summary: Confirm revoking all other sessions while keeping the current one; success summary of how many were signed out; and a failed state.
frames.acceptanceNotes: DELETE /sessions (all others, idempotent). Confirm shows the kept current device explicitly. Done state reports the count of revoked sessions. The current session remains valid throughout.
